description_long: This is a detailed stone sculpture depicting the Hindu deity Ganesha, characterized by an elephant head with a prominent trunk, multiple arms, and ornate headdress. The figure stands on a rounded pedestal base, with intricate carvings visible on the body, arms, and headgear. The front view reveals expressive facial features, jewelry, and symbolic objects held in the deity's hands. The surface is weathered, with a rough, matte finish and visible signs of age and patina. The back of the sculpture is less detailed, with the outline of the headdress and shoulders visible but minimal ornamentation. The overall form is solid and robust, suggesting it is carved from a single block of stone.
description: A weathered stone sculpture of the Hindu deity Ganesha, featuring an elephant head, multiple arms, and ornate carvings, standing on a rounded pedestal.
description_view_0: Side view showing the profile of Ganesha's head, trunk, and arms, with the layered headdress and rounded base visible.
description_view_1: Frontal view displaying the full face, trunk, multiple arms, detailed jewelry, and symbolic objects, with the pedestal base clearly seen.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the other profile of the head, trunk, and arms, with the headdress and base visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the headdress and shoulders, with minimal detail and a smooth, weathered surface.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible; the sculpture is isolated against a plain background.
